input.prompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

input.initialPrompt:
I'm starting a new .NET MAUI app that needs a flyout menu with two sections:
"Dashboard" (single page) and "Products" (with two sub-tabs: "Active" and
"Archived"). I also need a Settings page accessible from the flyout.
Show me how to set up AppShell.xaml with this structure, including route
registration for a ProductDetailsPage that I'll navigate to from the list.

data.content:
Here's the full setup:

**AppShell.xaml**

```xml
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" ?>
<Shell
    x:Class="MyMauiApp.AppShell"
    xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
    xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
    xmlns:pages="clr-namespace:MyMauiApp.Pages"
    FlyoutBehavior="Flyout">

    <!-- Dashboard — single page, no tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Dashboard" Icon="icon_dashboard.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:DashboardPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Products — two tabs -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Products" Icon="icon_products.png">
        <Tab Title="Active">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ActiveProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
        <Tab Title="Archived">
            <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:ArchivedProductsPage}" />
        </Tab>
    </FlyoutItem>

    <!-- Settings -->
    <FlyoutItem Title="Settings" Icon="icon_settings.png">
        <ShellContent ContentTemplate="{DataTemplate pages:SettingsPage}" />
    </FlyoutItem>

</Shell>
```

**AppShell.xaml.cs**

```csharp
namespace MyMauiApp;

public partial class AppShell : Shell
{
    public AppShell()
    {
        InitializeComponent();

        // Register the detail route so you can navigate with:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(ProductDetailsPage));
        // or with a parameter:
        //   await Shell.Current.GoToAsync($"{nameof(ProductDetailsPage)}?id={product.Id}");
        Routing.RegisterRoute(nameof(Pages.ProductDetailsPage), typeof(Pages.ProductDetailsPage));
    }
}
```

**Key points:**

- Each `FlyoutItem` becomes one flyout menu entry. Wrapping content in multiple `<Tab>` elements gives you the bottom tab bar under "Products".
- `ContentTemplate` with `DataTemplate` defers page creation until the user navigates there.
- `Routing.RegisterRoute` enables programmatic navigation to pages **not** declared in the visual hierarchy (like a detail/push page).
- To navigate: `await Shell.Current.GoToAsync(nameof(ProductDetailsPage));`
- To pass data, implement `IQueryAttributable` on `ProductDetailsPage` or use `[QueryProperty]`.
